text: Being here among others who, like me, seem immersed in their own thoughts and struggles, I feel a strange camaraderie. There is comfort in our shared effort, a quiet dignity in each person’s presence. The words about sacrifice and appreciation resonate. Sometimes, I sense the weight of having to carry on, but observing others, I also remember that we are all enduring, sometimes celebrating, sometimes simply surviving. There is bittersweetness: my own difficulty feels less lonely amidst the collective.
